title: orobanche alsatica var. laserpitii-sileris
intro: Orobanche alsatica var. laserpitii-sileris, also known as Siler broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that belongs to the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae). It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Siler broomrape include relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, and it has also been used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Siler broomrape is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it can also be used in the form of extracts or infusions in traditional medicine.
